results.title: Vela: AI scheduling that works the way a great EA does - Product Hunt
results.content:
Saatvik
Vela

Vela

@vik\_sh thanks, Victor. Vela is super smart. It looks at your previous calendar history in order to decide what you have done before. And if there's no clear evidence, it escalates to a human, until eventually it learns all of your priorities and preferences perfectly.

Daniele Packard
ChatPal

ChatPal

Wow very powerful - integrates with google calendar?

Saatvik
Vela

Vela

@daniele\_packard Thanks Daniele!

Saatvik
Vela

Vela

@daniele\_packard Absolutely yes! Works across ALL email clients.

Nikola Djordjevic

Man, I get the scheduling chaos. We used to juggle three different tools just to keep things semi-organized. Vela sounds like a godsend for cutting that out.

Gobhanu Korisepati
Vela

Vela [...] It has taste and TRULY learns. Vela knows a board member outranks a coffee chat without you writing rules. It understands "early next week" without rigid parameters.  
It handles the mess. Ghosted? Vela follows up. Conflicting time zones? Handled. Multi-party interviews with 5 calendars? Done.  
  
We're already used by enterprise recruiters scheduling thousands of interviews a week and by founders who need white-glove coordination for their highest-stakes meetings.  
We're currently running paid pilots and yearly contracts with individuals and businesses. If scheduling is a pain point for you, feel free to book a demo - we'd love to show you what Vela can do. [...] Gobhanu Korisepati
Vela

Vela

@djordjevic\_nikola appreciate it! Would love to get you setup and show you a Demo. We promise Vela can literally do anything a human would with regards to scheduling :) extremely consistently!

Saatvik
Vela

Vela

@djordjevic\_nikola Ahhh thank you Nikola! Means the world to us... cant wait to have you try out Vela!

Pranjali Awasthi
Slashy

Slashy

Genuinely hate checking my calendar, Vela has taken that obligation out of my day to day

Saatvik
Vela

Vela

@pranjali\_awasthi thank you so much, Anjali. It's been a pleasure working with you.

Saatvik
Vela

Vela

We are not going to rest until 'let me check my calendar' is a phrase nobody ever has to say again

Gobhanu Korisepati

Vela

@apexflux lets gooooo

results.title: VELA: Securely execute AI-generated & untrusted code - Product Hunt
results.content:
exec() or subprocess on the host machine? Terrifying. One bad prompt injection and the LLM accesses your .env files or exfiltrates data.

Standard Docker containers? Too slow and heavy for rapid-fire agent tool calls.

Cloud sandboxes? Great, but I didn't want to send my local data or proprietary agent logic to a 3rd party API just to run a simple pandas script.

0

2

## Forum Threads

VELA
Praveen

2h ago

### How are you safely executing code generated by your AI agents?

Hey Product Hunt community!

As a solo maker, I've been diving deep into the world of autonomous AI agents (LangChain, LlamaIndex, etc.). One of the biggest bottlenecks I kept hitting was code execution.

When an agent needs to analyze data, scrape a site, or run a simulation, it writes code. But where do you safely run it? [...] Praveen
Lineage Lens

Lineage Lens

Sanjay Kumar

The Firecracker micro-VM approach makes sense here, the ~150ms cold start seems totally workable for tool call use cases. I run a lot of AI-generated code via Claude Code and this is exactly the kind of safety layer I'd want underneath it. Does it handle MCP tool call contexts or is it mainly focused on raw script execution right now? Congrats on shipping!

results.title: Wideframe: AI Coworker for Video Editors | Product Hunt
results.content:
Jeff Morris Jr.

Antimetal

Congrats Daniel and ZK on the launch. It's been a pleasure to see two of my favorite people build this together. Video is the dominant communication form in the world today, and your product looks so great!

Daniel Pearson

@jmj Thanks, Jeff!

Gobhanu Korisepati
Vela

Vela

wow this is insane! what LLM providers do you guys use for the editing?

Daniel Pearson

@gobhanu\_korisepati Our agent has access to a ton of different tools for search, video understanding, sequencing, etc... but special shouts to Anthropic and Gemini models! We lean on them a lot. It's crazy how often SOTA is changing hands in video models though...

Mike Sykulski
